meaning: very wise (describing Allah in this ayah)
and follow intensely! (command to follow what is being revealed to you)
and put your trust in! (command to one person)
more/most just (than calling them by other than their fathers' names)
and/then if (connects this condition to previous statement)
very forgiving (describing Allah in this ayah)
and his spouses (referring to the Prophet's wives who are mothers to the believers)
their mothers (referring to the Prophet's wives being like mothers to the believers)
more worthy/closer (to the believers than themselves)
and the specific group of emigrants (affected by previous word)
something well-known and widely accepted as good
that (referring to what was mentioned before)
the prophets (who Allah took a covenant from)
and from (connecting to previous prophets mentioned)
from them (referring to the prophets mentioned)
the truthful ones (the ones being asked about)
and he prepared (for the disbelievers a painful punishment)
they believed (referring to the believers being addressed)
so we caused to be sent
when (referring to the time when the enemy forces came)
and when (introducing what the hypocrites said)
their hearts (referring to the hypocrites and those with disease in their hearts)
nothing but deception (what Allah and His Messenger promised, according to the hypocrites)
a group (that is separate from others)
running away (as the object being discussed)
it was entered (referring to the cities)
they were asked (referring to a group of people)
they would surely give/bring it (feminine object)
except (what comes after this word is excluded from what was mentioned before)
you all ran away (referring to a group being addressed in this ayah)
they find (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier)
for them (referring to those who try to protect others from Allah)
the specific people who prevent others
and not (they do not come to battle)
a little, a few (describing how much they come to battle)
he is being overwhelmed (like one who faints from death)
they attacked you all with sharp words
those (referring to the hypocrites mentioned before)
and if (connecting this condition to the previous statement)
the last (referring to the Day of Judgment)
they changed/substituted (referring to those who stayed true to their covenant)
the people who truly tell the truth (who Allah will reward)
very merciful (describing Allah as a characteristic)
with their rage (describing how Allah sent them back)
and He was sufficient (Allah being sufficient for the believers against their enemies)
very strong (describing Allah in this ayah)
and he sent down (referring to Allah sending down the people of the book from their fortresses)
and he made you all inherit
their land (what Allah made the believers inherit)
I will provide much enjoyment to you (feminine plural)
and I will release you (feminine plural)
easy (describing how something is for Allah)
we give her (in this ayah: We will give her reward twice)
you (feminine plural) submit (addressing the Prophet's wives)
and then he desires/covets (in the hearts of those who have disease)
and he thoroughly purifies you all
Most Subtle (describing Allah in this ayah)
and the devoted and obedient women
and the truthful ones (plural for men)
and the female ones who fast
and female ones who remember intensely
for them (referring to the believing men and women mentioned earlier)
a complete forgiveness (as something Allah has prepared)
their matter/affair (referring to the believers mentioned earlier)
he disobeys (in a command or condition)
then certainly (connects to previous statement about disobeying Allah)
upon him (referring to the one Allah blessed)
and protect yourself (command to one person)
and Allah (as the subject doing something)
then when (connects this completed action to what follows)
those who (plural, referring to people in the past)
they passed away (referring to those who came before)
they are afraid (referring to those who deliver Allah's messages)
as a thorough reckoner (describing Allah in this ayah)
and glorify Him! (as a command to a group)
so that he makes you all go out
they meet him (on the Day they meet Him)
and he prepared (for them a reward)
and as a bearer of good news
the believing women (who are being married)
then (connects this part about divorce to the previous part about marriage)
a releasing/divorce (as manner of doing it)
we made (something) required (referring to marriage rules in this ayah)
so that not/in order that not
that (pointing to what was just mentioned)
he should feel sad (in context: they should not feel sad)
very gentle/patient (describing Allah in this ayah)
their beauty (referring to the women mentioned)
ones who watch/observe (referring to believers being addressed)
so all of you enter! (after being invited)
ones (plural men) who are seeking comfort/familiarity (with the Prophet's presence)
that (pointing to something far) for you all
his wives (whom you may not marry)
forever (describing how long something will not happen)
that (pointing to something far) for all of you
great/magnificent (describing how serious this matter was to Allah)
and not (connecting to previous items in the list)
and protect yourselves (command for group of females)
Allah (as the object being witnessed in this verse)
they send blessings intensely (referring to Allah and His angels)
O you (calling to those who believe)
they believed (referring to the believers being addressed)
and all of you make peace! (intensively)
Allah (as the object being harmed in this verse)
they harm (referring to those who harm believers)
clearly, manifestly (describing how they carried a burden in this ayah)
and so not (they will not be harmed)
constantly forgiving (describing Allah in this ayah)
extremely kind and caring (describing Allah in this ayah)
he stops himself (in a conditional phrase)
their hearts (referring to the hypocrites and those with disease in their hearts)
and the ones who spread harmful rumors
a little while (describing how long they will stay)
those who are completely rejected (from mercy)
the people (who ask about the Hour)
its knowledge (referring to the Hour/Day of Judgment)
forever (describing how long they will stay in it)
they find (referring to the disbelievers in Hell)
they are being turned over repeatedly
they are saying (referring to the disbelievers in the Fire)
so they made us lose our way
bring to them! (command to our Lord)
those who (describing the believers who are being addressed)
they believed (referring to the believers being addressed)
and so he cleared him from blame
and he was (connects this phrase to the previous part of the ayah)
Allah (the one true God who is being addressed)
